measures.what_it_does: Restores Fire Protection District No. 6's regular property-tax levy in 2027 to no more than $1.00 per $1,000, permits a 103% annual levy limit factor for nine years, and makes the final maximum the base for later levy limits.
measures.cost_line: At the maximum stated rate, $100 annually per $100,000 of assessed value, or $500 on a $500,000 property; later bills depend on levy and assessed value.
measures.pro_summary: No pro-committee statement was available; the official ballot says the measure restores the district's regular levy for local fire-protection funding.
measures.con_summary: No con-committee statement was available; the direct trade-off is higher and potentially growing property-tax collections versus district fire-protection revenue.

taxes.basis: YES restores the levy up to $1.00 per $1,000, permits up to 3% annual levy growth for nine years, and permanently resets the later levy base.

measures.sources_note: Official sample-ballot title (S1); no pro or con committee statements were available in the reviewed election sources.
